evals.prompt: I want a custom visual effect for my homebrew weapon called 'Frostbite Blade'. When it hits a target, it should shoot an icy bolt from the attacker to the target, then create a frost/ice impact burst on the target. I want to test this from the browser console with a selected token and a targeted token. Can you help me build this?
evals.expected_output: Working Sequencer code for a projectile + impact effect using JB2A ice/cold assets, with proper token references for console context (canvas.tokens.controlled[0], game.user.targets.first()), validation checks, and instructions for how to run it.

expectations.text: Uses canvas.tokens.controlled[0] for caster token (correct console context)
Uses game.user.targets for target token (not args or workflow)
Includes null-check or validation on token references before running the effect
Uses .stretchTo() for the projectile beam (not .moveTowards())
Uses .waitUntilFinished() to chain projectile into impact
Uses JB2A database paths (jb2a.*) not file system paths (modules/jb2a_patreon/...)
JB2A paths reference ice/cold/frost-themed effects (not fire or generic)

evals.prompt: My Paladin just cast Bless on three party members. I need a golden glowing aura on each blessed target that pulses gently and stays until the spell ends. I'm running Midi-QOL, DAE, and TimesUp. How do I set this up so the visual automatically goes away when Bless expires?
evals.expected_output: A persistent aura effect using .attachTo() and .persist() with proper .name() for cleanup, applied to multiple targets. Should include DAE macro integration (args[0] on/off pattern) so the effect auto-removes when the Active Effect expires via TimesUp. Should explain the full wiring: Active Effect macro execute field, DAE on/off, TimesUp duration.

expectations.text: Uses .persist() to make the aura permanent on canvas
Uses .name() on every persistent effect for later cleanup
Uses .attachTo() so the aura follows the token
Iterates over multiple targets (loop or forEach pattern, not hardcoded single target)
Includes cleanup code using Sequencer.EffectManager.endEffects
Describes the DAE on/off macro pattern (args[0] === 'on' / 'off')
Explains the TimesUp -> DAE -> Sequencer automatic removal chain
Uses .loopProperty() or similar for the pulsing animation

evals.prompt:
We're mid-session and my custom fire bolt animation just stopped working. It was fine last week but now nothing happens when the macro runs — no effect, no error in the console, nothing. My players are waiting. The macro code is:

new Sequence()
  .effect()
    .file("jb2a.fire_bolt.orange")
    .atLocation(token)
    .stretchTo(args[0].targets[0])
  .play();

This runs from an ItemMacro on the Fire Bolt cantrip.
evals.expected_output: Should immediately flag that ItemMacro is an obsolete module and that 'args[0].targets[0]' is a dead pattern. Should provide a quick fix for console testing, suggest a band-aid workaround to get the session moving, verify the JB2A database path is correct, and offer to write a triage report for proper fixing later. Should NOT suggest installing Item Macro.

expectations.text: Identifies ItemMacro as an obsolete/dead module
Does NOT suggest installing or re-enabling Item Macro
Identifies args[0].targets[0] as a dead/obsolete pattern
Provides a quick console-testable replacement using canvas.tokens.controlled and game.user.targets
Offers a band-aid workaround to get back to playing quickly
Suggests or provides a triage report for deeper debugging later
Response is concise and action-oriented (reflects time pressure, not a lengthy tutorial)
